title_en: Some Scottish wrens are a striking case of “island syndrome”
title_zh: 一些苏格兰鹪鹩是“岛屿综合征”的鲜明例子
rubric_en: They have got much bigger than their mainland cousins
rubric_zh: 它们比本土同类大得多

paragraphs.en: Animals living on islands can develop strange characteristics. (Just look at the British.) Isolated, safe from mainland predators and facing less competition for resources, island-dwellers often evolve differently from mainland relatives. Some develop “island syndromes”. For example, small animals get bigger and big animals get smaller. The dodo of Mauritius evolved from the size of a pigeon to that of a large turkey, losing the use of its wings along the way.
paragraphs.zh: 生活在岛屿上的动物常常会长出一些奇特的特征。（看看不列颠人就行了。）远离大陆、免受大陆捕食者威胁，并且面临较少的资源竞争，岛屿生物往往会与大陆近亲走上不同的进化道路。有些会形成所谓“岛屿综合征”。例如，小型动物变大，而大型动物变小。毛里求斯的渡渡鸟就是一个例子，它从鸽子大小演化到了像大只火鸡那么大，并在这个过程中失去了使用翅膀的能力。

paragraphs.en: Some wrens in Scotland have developed the syndrome. Cut off from mainland Britain for thousands of years, two subspecies on St Kilda and Shetland, a pair of archipelagoes, have evolved to be much larger than mainland wrens, says a recent paper in the Evolutionary Journal of the Linnean Society.
paragraphs.zh: 苏格兰的一些鹪鹩也出现了这种综合征。来自斯特·基尔达岛和设得兰群岛的两个亚种被大陆上的祖先隔绝了几千年后，已演化得比大陆上的同类大得多，《Linnean学会进化杂志》最近的一篇论文是这样指出的。

paragraphs.en: Researchers set mist nets beside speakers playing recordings of wren song across Shetland, Fair Isle, the Outer Hebrides and St Kilda—a method to lure birds out of cover. “Catching them was a nightmare,” says Michal Jezierski, the paper’s lead author. Wrens are so territorial that when song played, the birds simply sang back, refusing to budge. Still, the team caught 45 wrens, enough to study systematically. Using data of historical specimens from the National Museums Scotland, and body-mass records from the British Trust for Ornithology, they compared the island wrens with their mainland relatives—and with 695 other island-mainland bird pairs.
paragraphs.zh: 研究人员在设得兰、费尔岛、外赫布里底群岛和斯特·基尔达岛附近设置了带有播放鹪鹩鸣叫录音的诱捕网，这是一种引鸟离开隐蔽处的方法。论文第一作者Michal Jezierski说：“捕它们简直是一场噩梦。”鹪鹩的领地意识非常强，一听到鸣叫，它们往往只是回应鸣唱，纹丝不动。尽管如此，研究团队仍然捕到45只鹪鹩，足以进行系统研究。他们使用了苏格兰国家博物馆的历史标本数据，以及来自英国鸟类学会的体重记录，将岛屿鹪鹩与大陆同类——以及695对其他“岛屿-大陆”鸟类——进行比较。

paragraphs.en: Fair Isle wrens were a similar size to mainland ones, and those on the Outer Hebrides were a tad larger. But St Kilda and Shetland wrens were gigantic: the largest was twice as heavy as the smallest mainland wren. They are in the top 25% most extreme cases of “island gigantism” recorded, much like New Zealand’s kakapo, the world’s heaviest parrot. They also had distinctive songs.
paragraphs.zh: 费尔岛的鹪鹩体型与大陆鹪鹩大致相当，外赫布里底群岛的则略大一些。但斯特·基尔达和设得兰的鹪鹩则巨大无比：最大的鹪鹩体重是最小大陆鹪鹩的两倍。它们属于已记录的“岛屿巨型化”中最极端的前25%案例之一，和新西兰的卡卡波相似——卡卡波是世界上最重的鹦鹉。它们的鸣唱也颇具辨识度。

paragraphs.en: Only a small share of the genes that govern size overlapped in the two bird populations, despite them being similarly large. This suggests they evolved in parallel, via different genetic paths. It is still unclear why island animals become giants (or dwarfs). But Scottish wrens provide a piece of the puzzle. ■
paragraphs.zh: 尽管这两类鸟群体型相近，调控体型的基因重叠比例却很小，这表明它们是沿着不同的遗传路径并行进化而来。为何岛屿动物会成为巨型（或侏儒）目前仍不清楚，但苏格兰鹪鹩为这一谜题提供了线索。■

paragraphs.en: For more expert analysis of the biggest stories in Britain, sign up to Blighty, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter.
paragraphs.zh: 如需获取更多关于英国重大新闻的专家深度解读，请订阅《Blighty》——我们的每周付费新闻通讯。
